source: entity2rec- Learning User-Item Relatedness from Knowledge Graphs for Top-N Item Recommendation

concepts.finding: Knowledge-graph-based recommendation using entity embeddings. Learns user-item relatedness through graph paths in a knowledge graph (movies linked to actors, genres, directors). Meta-path-based features (count, type, length of paths) combined with learning-to-rank. Outperforms collaborative filtering, especially for cold-start items.
concepts.implication: Keyword-intent relatedness can be computed via knowledge graph paths (keyword -> category -> intent). Graph path features (length, type, count) provide interpretable evidence for keyword-intent matching.
concepts.open_question: What knowledge graph entities and relationships are available for computing keyword-intent relatedness? Is a knowledge graph approach feasible without an existing KG?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Knowledge graph path features between keywords and intents outperform embedding similarity for keyword-intent assignment accuracy and explainability.
